situations in the present or future.

Structure

If ClauseMain

ClauseIf + Present Simplewill + base verb
If it rains, I will

stay at home.
If you study hard, you will pass the exam.

Important Notes

  • The if-clause

can come first or second.
  • Do NOT use will in the if-clause.
  • Other modals: can, might, should in the
answer.shouldIf he is late, we should start without him.

imaginary situations in the present or future.

Structure

If ClauseMain

ClauseIf + Past Simplewould + base verb
If I had more money, I

would travel the world.
If she were taller, she would be a model.

Important Notes

  • Use

were (not was) with all subjects in formal writing.
  • The situation is NOT real ??? imaginary or
(hypothetical)If I have time, I will come.If I had time, I would come.

column3:
Passive Voice

Use the Passive when the action is more

column1:
important than who performs it, or when the doer is unknown.

Structure: be + past
cleans the room.The room is cleaned.Past SimpleThey built this
bridge.This bridge was built.Present PerfectHe has written the
report.The report has been written.Future (will)They will finish
the project.The project will be finished.ModalYou must sign the

form.The form must be signed.

How to Transform Active to

Passive
  1. Object of active ??? subject of passive.
  2. Correct form of be for the

tense.
  • Main verb ??? past participle.
  • Add by + agent if

needed.
Active: Tom wrote the letter.
Passive: The letter was written by Tom.
